item.title: Charters - the court ruling is a win for journalism in Fiji, I'm delighted that the burden of the FICAC prosecution has been lifted off me
Team Fiji bowler, Prasad upsets reigning Bowls World Cup champion Sam Tolchard in Glasgow opener
item.description: Team Fiji’s bowling squad made a perfect start to their Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games campaign, with Rajnesh Prasad producing a historic upset in the men’s singles Section C at the SEC Centre. Prasad defeated England’s reigning Bowls World Cup champion Sam Tolchard 8-3, 6-7, 1-0 in a thrilling tie-break decider, marking the first time bowls has been contested indoors in the Commonwealth Games hi
item.content:encoded:
Team Fiji’s bowling squad made a perfect start to their Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games campaign, with Rajnesh Prasad producing a historic upset in the men’s singles Section C at the SEC Centre.

Prasad defeated England’s reigning Bowls World Cup champion Sam Tolchard 8-3, 6-7, 1-0 in a thrilling tie-break decider, marking the first time bowls has been contested indoors in the Commonwealth Games history.

The Fijian dominated the opening set 8-3 at the Siti Zaliha Ahmad Rink before Tolchard, a four-time Commonwealth Games medallist competing at his fifth Games, fought back to take the second set 7-6.

Prasad remained composed under pressure, however, securing the decisive single point in the tie-break to claim a memorable victory.

Fiji’s women’s pair also enjoyed a strong opening day, completing a perfect outing for the Pacific side with an 11-1, 4-4 victory over Botswana on the adjacent rink.

The men’s singles campaign continues tomorrow with a second-round clash against Jamaica’s Robert Simpson, while the women’s pair will face Wales tomorrow morning.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Excavator blocking Suva footpath raises safety concerns
item.description: An excavator blocking a public footpath along Selbourne Street in Suva has raised safety concerns, particularly for pedestrians and school students who use the route to access Suva's central business district. The excavator's arm is extending over the footpath, obstructing pedestrian access and posing a potential safety hazard.
item.content:encoded:
An excavator blocking a public footpath along Selbourne Street in Suva has raised safety concerns, particularly for pedestrians and school students who use the route to access Suva's central business district.

The excavator's arm is extending over the footpath, obstructing pedestrian access and posing a potential safety hazard.

fijivillage News has contacted the Suva City Council regarding the matter.

The Council has confirmed that a team has been deployed to assess the situation and address the issue.

The Council says officers will also meet with the owner of the excavator to ensure the obstruction is removed and public safety is maintained.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Trial date to be set in 4.7kg meth case on Nov 6th
item.description: The trial date for four men charged in connection with the alleged importation of 4.7 kilograms of methamphetamine is expected to be set on November 6th. Lawyer Amani Bale withdrew from representing Sevuloni Lubi and Eroni Bati after the Legal Aid Commission came on board to represent them. Suva High Court Judge Justice Daniel Goundar says all pre-trial litigation should be completed before Novemb
item.content:encoded:
The trial date for four men charged in connection with the alleged importation of 4.7 kilograms of methamphetamine is expected to be set on November 6th. Lawyer Amani Bale withdrew from representing Sevuloni Lubi and Eroni Bati after the Legal Aid Commission came on board to represent them. Suva High Court Judge Justice Daniel Goundar says all pre-trial litigation should be completed before November 6th, as he is expected to set a trial date. Bail was extended for all four. Ratu Lote Leweivuna and Sevuloni Lubi are charged with unlawful possession of illicit drugs, Eroni Bati is charged with the unlawful importation of illicit drugs, while Alipate Matadigo is charged with the transportation of illicit drugs.

item.title: Mother renews call for improved search response after private search fails to find son
item.description: The mother of missing 20-year-old Alivereti Vunakece is renewing her call for improvements to Fiji's search and rescue response after a privately organised search with volunteer divers failed to locate her son, while Police say poor weather affected their search efforts. Kelera Seniloli says although the search ended without finding her son, she now has peace of mind knowing she did everything pos
item.content:encoded:
The mother of missing 20-year-old Alivereti Vunakece is renewing her call for improvements to Fiji's search and rescue response after a privately organised search with volunteer divers failed to locate her son, while Police say poor weather affected their search efforts.

Kelera Seniloli says although the search ended without finding her son, she now has peace of mind knowing she did everything possible to bring him home.

Vunakece disappeared on 10 July while snorkelling near Naevuevu Village in Sigatoka after he was caught in a strong current.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Seniloli says after official search efforts ended, her family organised its own search with the help of volunteer divers and community members.

She says the divers carried out several searches in the area where her son went missing, guided by her husband, who is familiar with the waters and underwater caves.

Despite hours of searching and multiple dives, they were unable to locate Vunakece.

Seniloli is now calling for improvements to Fiji's search and rescue response, saying specialist teams such as the Republic of Fiji Navy and trained search and rescue units should be deployed immediately when incidents occur at known drowning hotspots instead of relying solely on local resources.

She also expressed her heartfelt gratitude to Aju Naidu and Sitiveni Gaunavinaka of Naidu's Adventure for providing the boat used in the search, and to George Smith, Ashwin Pal and his son Masi Pal, who was Alivereti's classmate, from Diveaway Fiji at Hideaway Resort on the Coral Coast, for volunteering their time and expertise to help search for her son.

Responding to questions from fijivillage News, Police confirmed that Vunakece remains missing.

Police say the initial report was received after 6pm, and officers were unable to begin a sea search that evening because of darkness.

They say search operations the following day were assisted by officers from the Police Mobile Force dive unit and resort staff using a boat.

Police also say unfavourable weather conditions later forced the sea search to be suspended, with officers continuing searches on land while monitoring weather conditions before any further deployment.

Police have not yet responded to fijivillage News' question on whether the search is continuing.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Sweet Sensation ready to rock the CFL Gratitude Concert
item.description: Sweet Sensation is promising an energetic performance featuring everything from rock and reggae to R&B and Afro music when the band takes the stage at the Communications Fiji Limited (CFL) Gratitude Concert this Saturday. Band leader Olsen J Sefo says the group is fully prepared and excited to entertain concertgoers with a diverse setlist that reflects the band's musical versatility while staying
item.content:encoded:
Sweet Sensation is promising an energetic performance featuring everything from rock and reggae to R&B and Afro music when the band takes the stage at the Communications Fiji Limited (CFL) Gratitude Concert this Saturday.

Band leader Olsen J Sefo says the group is fully prepared and excited to entertain concertgoers with a diverse setlist that reflects the band's musical versatility while staying true to its roots.

He says while the band enjoys experimenting with different genres, they will never forget where it started.

Sefo describes performing at the CFL Gratitude Concert as both an honour and a blessing, saying the event celebrates local talent and recognises the role CFL has played in promoting Fijian music.

He says CFL has consistently supported local artists by giving their music airtime across its radio stations, helping showcase home-grown talent to audiences throughout the country.

He says the band is also looking forward to sharing the stage with some of Fiji's best-known musicians, adding that it is a privilege to perform alongside artists they admire.

The CFL Gratitude Concert will be held this Saturday at 1pm, with food stalls opening for families, while live performances will start at 3pm.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj
